texto: Elephant wanted no part of Rupert Panther. Rather, he wanted Rupert Panther to have no part of him, which was a realistic concern because Rupert was looking at Elephant like a gambler coming off a marathon poker game in Las Vegas looks at the all-you-can-eat breakfast buffet, like he has something to settle with a tall stack of pancakes, and he's all business and all fork.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a T.A. Young: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a humor. O trecho “Elephant wanted no part of Rupert Panther. Rather, he wanted Rupert Panther to have no part of hi…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, fantasy-literature, magic pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de humor e magic.

frase_original: Elephant wanted no part of Rupert Panther. Rather, he wanted Rupert Panther to have no part of him, which was a realistic concern because Rupert was looking at Elephant like a gambler coming off a marathon poker game in Las Vegas looks at the all-you-can-eat breakfast buffet, like he has something to settle with a tall stack of pancakes, and he's all business and all fork.
